source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
title: Trust Thyself
why_selected: A compact American statement of inner authority; good for appositions, imperatives, and abstract nouns.
text_en: Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string. Accept the place the divine providence has found for you, the society of your contemporaries, the connection of events. Great men have always done so, and confided themselves childlike to the genius of their age, betraying their perception that the absolutely trustworthy was seated at their heart, working through their hands, predominating in all their being.

glosses.hint: people living at the same time

scaffold: 信任[祈使] 你自己：每一颗心 震动[一般现在] 到 那根 铁弦。接受[祈使] 那个位置，神意 已经 找到[现在完成 has+pp] 给你，接受[省略祈使] 你的同时代人的社会，接受[省略祈使] 事件的连接。伟大的人 总是 已经 这样 做过[现在完成 have+pp]，并且 把他们自己 像孩子一样 托付[一般过去] 给 他们时代的天才，显露[现在分词 -ing] 他们的感知：那个绝对可信的东西 坐落[一般过去 被动感] 在 他们的心里，工作[现在分词 -ing] 通过 他们的手，在 他们全部存在中 占主导[现在分词 -ing]。

palette.usage: present perfect inside relative-like object phrase
object in a list after Accept
participial phrase explaining the prior clause

grammar: Two imperatives set the passage's command structure: Trust, Accept.
The long third sentence hangs on Great men have done + and confided + betraying.
Working and predominating are participial continuations, not new finite verbs.
note: English lets Emerson pile abstract nouns after imperatives; Chinese needs you to keep the command spine visible.

source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
title: A Foolish Consistency
why_selected: A sharp passage about changing one's mind; useful for aphorism, concession, and contrast.
text_en: A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds, adored by little statesmen and philosophers and divines. With consistency a great soul has simply nothing to do. He may as well concern himself with his shadow on the wall. Speak what you think now in hard words and to-morrow speak what to-morrow thinks in hard words again, though it contradict every thing you said to-day.

glosses.hint: a frightening little spirit; here, a mental bugbear

scaffold: 一种 愚蠢的 一致性 是[一般现在] 小心灵的 妖怪，被 小政治家、小哲学家和神学家 崇拜[过去分词 被动修饰]。对于 一致性，一个 伟大的灵魂 简直 没有什么 要做[一般现在 has nothing to do]。他 也完全可以 让自己 操心[情态 may + 原形] 墙上的影子。说出[祈使] 你 现在 想的东西，用 强硬的话；并且 明天 再说出[祈使] 明天 所想的东西，用 强硬的话，即使 它 矛盾[一般现在] 你 今天 说过[一般过去] 的一切。

palette.usage: has nothing to do with structure

grammar: The first sentence is an aphorism: subject + is + metaphor.
Adored by... is a reduced passive relative clause modifying hobgoblin.
Though introduces permission for contradiction, not an apology for error.
note: The English keeps hitting with short propositions; don't smooth away the shock of hobgoblin and hard words.

title: Live Deliberately
why_selected: A famous passage on reducing life to essentials; excellent for infinitive chains and purpose clauses.
text_en: I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life, and see if I could not learn what it had to teach, and not, when I came to die, discover that I had not lived. I did not wish to live what was not life, living is so dear; nor did I wish to practise resignation, unless it was quite necessary.

glosses.hint: acceptance of something bad as unavoidable

scaffold: 我 去了[一般过去] 到 树林，因为 我 希望[一般过去] 去 有意识地 活，去 只 直面 生活的 本质事实，并且 看看[原形 see] 是否 我 不能 学到[情态 could + 原形] 它 必须 教给[一般过去 had to teach] 我的东西，并且 不要 在 我 来到[一般过去] 死的时候，发现[原形 discover] 我 没有 活过[过去完成 had+pp]。我 不 希望[一般过去] 去 活 那个 不是[一般过去] 生活的东西，活着 是[一般现在] 如此 珍贵；我 也不 希望[一般过去] 去 实践 认命，除非 它 是[一般过去] 相当 必要的。

palette.usage: past perfect; life not truly lived before death

grammar: The first sentence is one long purpose chain after wished: to live, to front, and see, and not discover.
Had not lived is past perfect because the failure would be discovered at death.
Nor did I wish uses auxiliary inversion after a negative connector.
note: Chinese wants to break this into smaller clauses; English lets the purpose chain stay suspended after wished.

title: Quiet Desperation
why_selected: A short diagnostic paragraph about hidden unhappiness; good for passive, contrast, and abstract social language.
text_en: The mass of men lead lives of quiet desperation. What is called resignation is confirmed desperation. From the desperate city you go into the desperate country, and have to console yourself with the bravery of minks and muskrats. A stereotyped but unconscious despair is concealed even under what are called the games and amusements of mankind.
scaffold: 大多数 人 过着[一般现在] 安静绝望的 生活。被 称作[一般现在 被动] 认命的东西 是[一般现在] 被确认的 绝望。从 绝望的 城市 你 走进[一般现在] 绝望的 乡村，并且 不得不 安慰[have to + 原形] 你自己，用 水貂和麝鼠的 勇敢。一个 刻板的 但 无意识的 绝望 被隐藏[一般现在 被动] 甚至 在 那些 被称作[一般现在 被动] 人类的 游戏和娱乐 的东西下面。
grammar: What is called resignation is a subject clause, not a question.
Is concealed is passive; despair is hidden under games and amusements.
From the city into the country is a movement pattern carrying the same adjective desperate.
note: The sentence keeps repeating desperation in different containers; keep that repetition rather than replacing it with synonyms.

source: Frederick Douglass, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass (Project Gutenberg #23)
title: The Silver Trump of Freedom
why_selected: A passage where an abstract idea becomes an all-perceiving presence; useful for metaphor and repeated parallel structure.
text_en: The silver trump of freedom had roused my soul to eternal wakefulness. Freedom now appeared, to disappear no more forever. It was heard in every sound, and seen in every thing. It was ever present to torment me with a sense of my wretched condition. I saw nothing without seeing it, I heard nothing without hearing it, and felt nothing without feeling it.
scaffold: 自由的 银色号角 已经 唤醒[过去完成 had+pp] 我的灵魂 到 永恒的 清醒。自由 现在 显现[一般过去]，为了 不再 消失[不定式 to + 原形] 永远。它 被听见[一般过去 被动] 在 每一个声音里，并且 被看见[一般过去 被动] 在 每一个东西里。它 总是 在场[一般过去] 来 折磨[不定式 to + 原形] 我，用 对 我悲惨处境的 感觉。我 什么也 没有看见[一般过去] 而不 看见[without + -ing] 它，我 什么也 没有听见[一般过去] 而不 听见[without + -ing] 它，并且 什么也 没有感觉[一般过去] 而不 感觉[without + -ing] 它。

palette.usage: past perfect; awakening before the later perception

grammar: Had roused is past perfect; the awakening is already complete before the described state.
Was heard and seen are passives: freedom is perceived everywhere.
The final sentence repeats nothing without + -ing three times; the repetition is the force.
note: The English turns freedom into a sensory object; keep the repeated it instead of explaining it away.

source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, the Modern Prometheus (Project Gutenberg #84)
title: A Torrent of Light
why_selected: A passage about knowledge, creation, and dangerous ambition; good for modals, relative clauses, and abstract ethical vocabulary.
text_en: No one can conceive the variety of feelings which bore me onwards, like a hurricane, in the first enthusiasm of success. Life and death appeared to me ideal bounds, which I should first break through, and pour a torrent of light into our dark world. A new species would bless me as its creator and source; many happy and excellent natures would owe their being to me.
scaffold: 没有人 能 想象[情态 can + 原形] 那种 感情的 多样性，那个 多样性 推着[一般过去 bore] 我 向前，像 飓风一样，在 成功的 最初狂热中。生命和死亡 对我 显现为[一般过去] 理想的 界限，那个界限 我 应该 首先 冲破[情态 should + 原形]，并且 把 一股 光的洪流 倾注[省略 should + 原形] 进 我们黑暗的世界。一个 新物种 会 祝福[情态 would + 原形] 我，作为 它的创造者和源头；许多 快乐而优秀的 天性 会 欠[情态 would + 原形] 它们的存在 给 我。

palette.usage: bounds = limits, not boundaries on a map

palette.english: would owe their being to me
palette.chinese: 会把它们的存在归功于我

grammar: Which bore me onwards is a relative clause; its antecedent is variety of feelings.
Should first break through and pour share the same modal should.
The semicolon joins two consequences of creation: bless me and owe their being to me.
note: The English makes ambition sound luminous before it becomes dangerous; keep the grand verbs and modal future.

source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
title: Rejected Thoughts Return
text_en: In every work of genius we recognize our own rejected thoughts; they come back to us with a certain alienated majesty. Great works of art have no more affecting lesson for us than this. They teach us to abide by our spontaneous impression with good-humored inflexibility then most when the whole cry of voices is on the other side. Else to-morrow a stranger will say with masterly good sense precisely what we have thought and felt all the time, and we shall be forced to take with shame our own opinion from another.

glosses.hint: a sense of grandeur that feels distant or estranged
cheerful stubbornness; unyielding but not bitter
the general opinion or public outcry

glosses.word: take with shame our own opinion from another
glosses.hint: accept back one's own idea from someone else, feeling embarrassed
glosses.chinese: 羞愧地从别人那里取回自己的观点

scaffold: 在每一件[定语从句起点] genius 的作品中，我们 认出[一般现在] 我们自己的 被拒绝的 thoughts；它们 回来[一般现在] 到我们 带着 一种 疏远的 majesty。伟大的 艺术作品 有[一般现在] 没有 更 动人的 lesson 对于 我们 比 这个。它们 教[一般现在] 我们 坚持[不定式] 我们的 自发的 impression 带着 好脾气的 inflexibility 在 最 时候 当 整个 声音的呼喊 是[一般现在] 在 另一边。否则 明天 一个 陌生人 将说[一般将来] 带着 大师般的 好 sense 精确地 什么 我们 已经[过去完成] 想 和 感觉 所有 时间，并且 我们 将被迫[一般将来被动] 带着 羞愧 接受 我们自己的 意见 从 另一个。

palette.chinese: 在每一件天才的作品中
我们自己的被拒绝的想法
palette.usage: 宾语，rejected 是过去分词作定语
方式状语，修饰 come back

palette.english: have no more affecting lesson for us than this
palette.chinese: 没有比这更动人的教训给我们

palette.usage: 方式状语，修饰 abide by

palette.english: we have thought and felt all the time
we shall be forced to take

palette.chinese: 我们自己的意见从别人那里

grammar: 一般现在时用于表达普遍真理或事实，如 'we recognize', 'they come back', 'Great works have'。
比较结构 'no more ... than' 表示 '和……一样不'，这里强调没有比这更动人的教训。
时间状语从句 'when the whole cry of voices is on the other side' 使用一般现在时表示将来。
将来时 'will say' 和 'shall be forced' 表示对未来的预测。
note: 英语中定语从句后置（如 'thoughts that we rejected' 变为 'rejected thoughts' 前置），而中文习惯前置定语，但本练习要求按英语语序用中文词序，所以定语从句标记起点。
vocab_targets: take with shame our own opinion from another
why_selected: This is the doorway to Emerson: the thought you dismissed returns from another mind with majesty.
context_note: Before the famous commands, Emerson explains why private perception matters: your rejected thought may come back wearing another author’s authority.

source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
title: Whoso Would Be a Man
text_en: Whoso would be a man, must be a nonconformist. He who would gather immortal palms must not be hindered by the name of goodness, but must explore if it be goodness. Nothing is at last sacred but the integrity of your own mind. Absolve you to yourself, and you shall have the suffrage of the world.

glosses.hint: person who does not conform to established customs
symbol of victory or honor that lasts forever
glosses.chinese: 不朽的棕榈叶（象征荣誉）

glosses.hint: state of being whole and undivided
set free from guilt or responsibility

scaffold: 无论谁[情态动词 would] 想要成为[不定式 to be] 一个人, 必须[情态动词 must] 是[be] 一个不墨守成规者. 他[关系代词 who] 想要[情态动词 would] 收集[不定式 to gather] 不朽的棕榈叶, 不能[情态动词 must not] 被阻碍[被动 be hindered] 被[by] 善良的名字, 而[but] 必须[情态动词 must] 探索[if 从句] 它是否[if] 是[be] 善良. 没有东西[否定] 是[be] 最终神圣的[at last sacred] 除了[but] 你自己思想的完整性. 赦免[祈使句 absolve] 你自己[to yourself], 并且[and] 你[shall] 将拥有[have] 世界的赞同.

palette.usage: 古语，相当于 whoever，引导主语从句
情态动词 would + be，表示愿望
泛指真正的人，有男子气概的人
情态动词 must + be，表示必要性
名词，指不遵从常规的人
关系代词 who 引导定语从句，修饰 he
情态动词 would + gather，表示愿望
象征胜利或荣誉，palms 用复数
情态动词 must not + be + 过去分词，被动语态
介词短语，表示被...所阻碍
连词，表示转折，连接两个并列成分
if 引导的宾语从句，be 为虚拟语气（古语）
系表结构，at last 为状语

palette.english: the integrity of your own mind
palette.usage: 名词短语，of 表示所属
祈使句，absolve 为及物动词，to yourself 为间接宾语
情态动词 shall 表示承诺或必然结果
名词短语，suffrage 原指投票权，此处指支持

grammar: 情态动词 would 表示愿望，must 表示必要性，must not 表示禁止。
被动语态：must not be hindered by...，结构为情态动词 + be + 过去分词。
虚拟语气：if it be goodness 中 be 为古语虚拟式，现代英语用 is。
祈使句：Absolve you to yourself，动词原形开头，you 为宾语。
note: 注意英语中定语从句后置（He who...），而中文习惯前置；另外情态动词和被动语态的顺序要准确。
why_selected: The central command of the essay: integrity over borrowed goodness.
context_note: This comes after Emerson attacks social conformity; the question is whether goodness is real or only a name enforced by others.

title: Simplify, Simplify
text_en: Our life is frittered away by detail. An honest man has hardly need to count more than his ten fingers, or in extreme cases he may add his ten toes, and lump the rest. Simplicity, simplicity, simplicity! I say, let your affairs be as two or three, and not a hundred or a thousand; instead of a million count half a dozen, and keep your accounts on your thumb nail.

glosses.word: keep your accounts on your thumb nail
glosses.chinese: 用指甲记账（比喻极简记账）

scaffold: 我们的生命[一般现在被动]被 细节 浪费[一般现在被动]掉。一个诚实的人[一般现在]几乎 不需要 数 超过 他的十个手指，或者 在极端情况下 他[情态动词 may]可以 加上 他的十个脚趾，并且 把其余的 归拢[一般现在]。简单，简单，简单！我[一般现在]说，让 你的事务 是[一般现在虚拟] 两件或三件，而不是 一百件或一千件；代替 一百万 数[一般现在] 半打，并且 在 你的拇指指甲上 记[一般现在] 你的账目。

palette.usage: 名词，作介词 by 的宾语
情态动词或实义动词，此处为实义动词后接不定式
祈使句动词，后接宾语+不定式

grammar: 被动语态：be + 过去分词，如 is frittered away
情态动词 may 后接动词原形，如 may add
祈使句：动词原形开头，如 let, keep
比较结构：more than 表示超过
note: 中文语序通常将状语放在动词前，而英语常将状语放在句末或动词后，如 'on your thumb nail' 放在句末。
vocab_targets: keep your accounts on your thumb nail
why_selected: This is the operational version of “simplification until I can touch”: life is too detailed to navigate unless reduced.
context_note: Thoreau is not praising aesthetic minimalism; he is arguing that detail can wreck a life like a ship in bad weather.

title: Castles in the Air
text_en: I learned this, at least, by my experiment; that if one advances confidently in the direction of his dreams, and endeavors to live the life which he has imagined, he will meet with a success unexpected in common hours. In proportion as he simplifies his life, the laws of the universe will appear less complex, and solitude will not be solitude, nor poverty poverty, nor weakness weakness. If you have built castles in the air, your work need not be lost; that is where they should be. Now put the foundations under them.

glosses.chinese: 在平常时刻意想不到的

glosses.hint: dreams or plans that are unrealistic
base or support for a structure

scaffold: 我[一般过去]学到这个，至少，通过我的实验；[that-从句起点]如果一个人[一般现在]前进自信地[定语从句起点]在方向他的梦想，并且[一般现在]努力去生活[定语从句起点]那个生活[定语从句起点]他[过去完成 had+pp]想象，他[一般将来]遇到[定语从句起点]一个成功[定语从句起点]意外[定语从句起点]在平常时刻。在比例[定语从句起点]他[一般现在]简化他的生活，[定语从句起点]法律宇宙[一般将来]显得更少复杂，并且孤独[一般将来]不是孤独，也不贫穷[一般将来]不是贫穷，也不虚弱[一般将来]不是虚弱。如果你[现在完成]建造城堡在空中，你的工作[情态动词]需要不[被动]丢失；[表语从句起点]那是[定语从句起点]它们应该[情态动词]在的地方。现在[祈使]放地基在它们下面。

palette.usage: pronoun referring to 'where they should be'

grammar: Use simple present for general truths (e.g., 'advances', 'simplifies').
Future tense with 'will' for results (e.g., 'will meet', 'will appear').
Present perfect for past actions with relevance (e.g., 'has imagined', 'have built').
Parallel structure with 'nor' for negative lists (e.g., 'solitude will not be solitude, nor poverty poverty').
note: English places relative clauses after the noun (e.g., 'the life which he has imagined'), while Chinese puts modifiers before the noun.
why_selected: A precise answer to ambition: don’t abandon the vision; put foundations under it.
context_note: Near the end of Walden, Thoreau turns experiment into instruction: changing the life changes the laws around the life.

source: Frederick Douglass, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass (Project Gutenberg #23)
title: The Pathway from Slavery to Freedom
text_en: These words sank deep into my heart, stirred up sentiments within that lay slumbering, and called into existence an entirely new train of thought. It was a new and special revelation, explaining dark and mysterious things, with which my youthful understanding had struggled, but struggled in vain. I now understood what had been to me a most perplexing difficulty—to wit, the white man’s power to enslave the black man. From that moment, I understood the pathway from slavery to freedom.
scaffold: 这些词语 沉入[一般过去] 深深 进入 我的 心， 激起[一般过去] 情感 在 之内 那 躺[过去进行] 沉睡[现在分词 -ing]， 并且 召唤[一般过去] 进入 存在 一个 完全 新 的 思路。 它 是[一般过去] 一个 新 的 和 特别 的 启示， 解释[现在分词 -ing] 黑暗 和 神秘 的 事物， 与 之 我的 年轻 的 理解力 已经挣扎[过去完成 had+pp]， 但 挣扎[过去完成 had+pp] 徒劳。 我 现在 理解[一般过去] 什么 曾经是[一般过去] 对 我 一个 最 令人困惑 的 困难——即， 白人 的 力量 去 奴役[不定式] 黑人。 从 那 一刻， 我 理解[一般过去] 道路 从 奴隶制 到 自由。

palette.usage: 过去式动词，描述词语进入内心
过去式动词短语，引发情感
过去进行时结构，lay为过去式，slumbering为现在分词
过去式动词短语，使产生
名词短语，全新的思考方向
名词短语，指难以理解的事物
名词短语，年轻人的认知
过去完成时，表示过去的过去
名词短语，难以理解的难题

grammar: 过去时与过去完成时的区别：'had struggled' 表示在理解之前发生的挣扎，而 'sank', 'stirred up', 'called' 是连续的一般过去动作。
现在分词作伴随状语：'explaining' 修饰 'revelation'，表示启示的内容。
不定式作定语：'power to enslave' 中不定式修饰 'power'，表示奴役的力量。
note: 英语中定语从句和分词短语常后置，而中文习惯前置；本句 'sentiments within that lay slumbering' 中定语从句后置，翻译时需调整语序。
why_selected: A scene where a prohibition becomes a revelation: literacy is dangerous because it leads to freedom.
context_note: Douglass overhears his master explaining why he must not learn to read; the master accidentally reveals the system’s hidden logic.

source: Frederick Douglass, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass (Project Gutenberg #23)
title: The Columbian Orator
text_en: Just about this time, I got hold of a book entitled “The Columbian Orator.” Every opportunity I got, I used to read this book. Among much of other interesting matter, I found in it a dialogue between a master and his slave. The slave was represented as having run away from his master three times. The dialogue represented the conversation which took place between them, when the slave was retaken the third time.
scaffold: 大约 这个 时间，我 得到[一般过去] 了 一本 书 标题[过去分词] “The Columbian Orator”。每个 机会 我 得到[一般过去]，我 过去常常 读 这 本书。在 许多 其他 有趣 的 内容 中，我 发现[一般过去] 在 它 里面 一个 对话 在 一个 主人 和 他的 奴隶 之间。奴隶 被 描绘[被动] 为 已经 逃跑[过去完成 had+pp] 从 他的 主人 那里 三次。对话 描绘[一般过去] 了 谈话 那[定语从句起点] 发生[一般过去] 在 他们 之间，当 奴隶 被 重新抓获[被动] 第三 次。

palette.usage: 过去分词作后置定语，修饰书
时间状语从句，表示频率

palette.english: among much of other interesting matter
palette.chinese: 在许多其他有趣的内容中

palette.usage: 介词短语，连接两个名词
动词，常用被动 be represented as
介词短语，as + 动名词完成式
连词，引导时间状语从句
动词，常用被动 be retaken

grammar: 一般过去时：描述过去特定时间发生的事件，如 got, found, represented, took place。
过去完成时：表示在另一个过去动作之前已经完成的动作，如 had run away。
被动语态：was represented, was retaken，强调动作承受者。
used to：表示过去习惯性动作，后接动词原形。
note: 中文语序通常将时间状语放在句首，而英语中时间状语可放在句首或句末；定语从句在英语中后置，中文前置。
why_selected: Reading becomes rehearsal for argument: the book gives his own thoughts a public tongue.
context_note: After learning to read, Douglass finds a book of speeches and dialogues; it supplies language for thoughts he already had but could not yet utter.

source: Frederick Douglass, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass (Project Gutenberg #23)
title: They Gave Tongue
text_en: These were choice documents to me. I read them over and over again with unabated interest. They gave tongue to interesting thoughts of my own soul, which had frequently flashed through my mind, and died away for want of utterance. The reading of these documents enabled me to utter my thoughts, and to meet the arguments brought forward to sustain slavery; but while they relieved me of one difficulty, they brought on another even more painful than the one of which I was relieved.

glosses.hint: the act of speaking or expressing

scaffold: 这些[复数] 是[一般过去] 选择 文件 对我[来说]. 我 阅读[一般过去] 它们 一遍又一遍 带着 未减的 兴趣. 它们 给予[一般过去] 声音 对 有趣 想法 我 自己 灵魂 的, 这[定语从句起点] 经常 闪烁[过去完成 had+pp] 通过 我 的 头脑, 并且 消失[一般过去] 离开 因为 缺乏 表达. 阅读[动名词] 这些 文件 使[一般过去] 我 能够 表达 我 的 想法, 并且 应对 论点 带来[被动] 向前 来 支持 奴隶制; 但是 当 它们 解除[一般过去] 我 的 一个 困难, 它们 带来[一般过去] 在...上 另一个 甚至 更痛苦 比 那一个 我 被解除[被动] 的.

palette.usage: 动词短语，过去式，后接名词
关系代词，引导定语从句
动词短语，过去式，enable sb to do
动词原形，与 to 构成不定式
动词短语，过去分词，修饰 arguments
连词，引导时间状语从句
动词短语，过去式，relieve sb of sth
定语从句，被动语态，介词+关系代词

grammar: 过去完成时 'had flashed' 表示在另一个过去动作之前发生的动作。
被动语态 'was relieved' 表示主语是动作的承受者。
定语从句 'which had frequently flashed...' 修饰 thoughts，关系代词 which 作主语。
比较结构 'even more painful than' 用于比较两个事物。
note: 注意英语中定语从句后置，而中文通常前置；本句中的 'which' 从句需放在被修饰词之后。
why_selected: A perfect phrase for why reading matters: it gives tongue to thoughts that had flashed and died.
context_note: The text does not merely inform Douglass; it arms his consciousness with speech.

source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus (Project Gutenberg #84)
title: I Ought to Be Thy Adam
text_en: Remember that I am thy creature; I ought to be thy Adam, but I am rather the fallen angel, whom thou drivest from joy for no misdeed. Everywhere I see bliss, from which I alone am irrevocably excluded. I was benevolent and good; misery made me a fiend. Make me happy, and I shall again be virtuous.

glosses.chinese: 亚当（圣经中第一个人类）
驱赶（古语第二人称单数）

glosses.hint: in a way that cannot be changed

scaffold: 记住[祈使] 那[引导宾语从句] 我 是[一般现在] 你的 创造物；我 应该[情态] 是[原形] 你的 亚当，但 我 是[一般现在] 相当 那 堕落的天使，定语从句起点 你 驱赶[一般现在] 从 欢乐 因为 没有 过错。到处 我 看见[一般现在] 幸福，从 那 我 独自 被[被动] 不可挽回地 排除[过去分词]。我 是[一般过去] 仁慈 和 善良；痛苦 使[一般过去] 我 成为 恶魔。使[祈使] 我 快乐，并且 我 将[情态] 再次 是[原形] 有道德的。

palette.usage: 系动词原形，用于情态动词后
关系代词，引导定语从句，指代the fallen angel
古英语动词形式，一般现在时第二人称单数，相当于drive
关系代词+介词，引导定语从句
动词，一般过去时，后接宾语+宾补
祈使句动词，后接宾语+宾补

grammar: 古英语代词：thy（你的），thou（你），drivest（驱赶，第二人称单数动词形式）。
关系代词whom引导定语从句，指代人，在从句中作宾语。
被动语态：am excluded（被排除），结构为be+过去分词。
祈使句：Remember... Make... 动词原形开头，表命令或请求。
note: 注意古英语代词和动词变位与现代英语不同，以及关系代词whom在从句中作宾语的位置。
why_selected: The created being makes the ethical claim the creator refuses to hear.
context_note: The creature confronts Victor: if you made me, you owe me justice, not abandonment.

source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus (Project Gutenberg #84)
title: Pioneer a New Way
text_en: As he went on I felt as if my soul were grappling with a palpable enemy; one by one the various keys were touched which formed the mechanism of my being; chord after chord was sounded, and soon my mind was filled with one thought, one conception, one purpose. So much has been done, exclaimed the soul of Frankenstein—more, far more, will I achieve; treading in the steps already marked, I will pioneer a new way, explore unknown powers, and unfold to the world the deepest mysteries of creation.

glosses.hint: able to be touched or felt
a group of musical notes played together
to be the first to explore or develop
things that are secret or unknown

scaffold: 当 他 继续[一般过去] 时，我 感觉[一般过去] 好像 我的 灵魂 正在[过去进行] 与 一个 可触摸的 敌人 搏斗；一个 一个 地，各种 琴键 被[被动] 触碰[一般过去]，这些 琴键 形成[一般过去] 了 我 存在 的 机制；和弦 一个 接 一个 地 被[被动] 奏响[一般过去]，很快 我的 头脑 被[被动] 充满[一般过去] 了 一个 想法，一个 概念，一个 目的。如此 多 的 事情 已经[现在完成] 被[被动] 完成[现在完成]，弗兰肯斯坦 的 灵魂 呼喊[一般过去] 道——更多，远 更 多，我 将[将来] 实现[将来]；踏 在 已经[现在完成] 被[被动] 标记[现在完成] 的 步骤 上，我 将[将来] 开辟[将来] 一条 新 路，探索[将来] 未知 的 力量，并 向 世界 揭示[将来] 创造 的 最 深 奥秘。

palette.usage: 动词短语，表示继续说话或行动
连词，引导方式状语从句，从句用虚拟语气
动词，被动，过去分词作定语

grammar: as if 从句用虚拟语气：were grappling（过去进行时虚拟）表示与当时事实相反。
被动语态：were touched, was sounded, was filled, has been done, are marked 等。
将来时：will achieve, will pioneer, will explore, will unfold。
现在分词作状语：treading in the steps... 表示伴随动作。
note: 英语中被动语态使用频繁，而中文多用主动形式；注意英语中定语从句后置（which formed...）和分词短语作定语（already marked）的位置。
why_selected: The intoxicating voice of discovery before responsibility appears.
context_note: A professor’s lecture ignites Victor; the sentence is ambition becoming a vow.

source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus (Project Gutenberg #84)
title: Dangerous Knowledge
text_en: I see by your eagerness and the wonder and hope which your eyes express, my friend, that you expect to be informed of the secret with which I am acquainted; that cannot be; listen patiently until the end of my story, and you will easily perceive why I am reserved upon that subject. I will not lead you on, unguarded and ardent as I then was, to your destruction and infallible misery. Learn from me, if not by my precepts, at least by my example, how dangerous is the acquirement of knowledge and how much happier that man is who believes his native town to be the world, than he who aspires to become greater than his nature will allow.
scaffold: 我[一般现在]看见 通过 你的 热切 和 那 惊奇 和 希望 那[定语从句起点] 你的 眼睛 表达[一般现在]，我的 朋友，那[that-从句] 你 期望[一般现在] 被[被动] 告知 关于 那 秘密 用 那 我[一般现在] 被[被动] 熟悉；那[that] 不能[情态] 是；听[祈使] 耐心地 直到 那 结束 的 我的 故事，和 你[一般将来] 容易地 察觉 为什么 我[一般现在] 是 保留 关于 那 主题。我[一般将来] 不 引导 你 继续，无防备 和 热切 像 我 那时[一般过去] 是，到 你的 毁灭 和 必然的 痛苦。学习[祈使] 从 我，如果 不 通过 我的 教训，至少 通过 我的 例子，如何 危险[一般现在] 是 那 获得 的 知识 和 如何 更 快乐 那 男人[一般现在] 是 谁[定语从句起点] 相信[一般现在] 他的 出生 城镇 是 那 世界，比 他 谁[定语从句起点] 渴望[一般现在] 成为 更 伟大 比 他的 本性 将[情态] 允许。

palette.english: I will not lead you on
how dangerous is the acquirement of knowledge
palette.chinese: 如何危险是那获得的知识

palette.english: how much happier that man is
who believes his native town to be the world
palette.chinese: 谁相信他的出生城镇是那世界

palette.english: than he who aspires to become greater than his nature will allow
palette.chinese: 比他谁渴望成为更伟大比他的本性将允许

grammar: Present tense for general truths and states (e.g., 'I see', 'you expect', 'I am acquainted').
Future tense with 'will' for predictions and promises (e.g., 'you will easily perceive', 'I will not lead you on').
Passive voice: 'to be informed', 'I am acquainted', 'I am reserved' — note the use of 'be' + past participle.
Relative clauses with 'which' and 'who' — note that the relative pronoun can be object of a preposition ('with which').
note: English often places relative clauses immediately after the noun they modify, while Chinese prefers to put them before the noun. Also, English uses inverted word order in exclamations like 'how dangerous is...'.
why_selected: The book’s warning in one sentence: knowledge can enlarge you beyond what your nature can bear.
context_note: Victor is narrating after catastrophe; this is not anti-learning, but a warning about unguarded ambition.
